title: Tulipa biebersteiniana
taxonomy: Tulipa biebersteiniana belongs to the family Liliaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the lily family. It is a species of lily that is native to the Caucasus region. Within the Tulipa genus, it is classified as a type of spring-flowering tulip. The species is further classified as a bulbous perennial plant, characterized by its distinctive cup-shaped flowers. Tulipa biebersteiniana is considered a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.
uses: Tulipa biebersteiniana, a perennial bulbous plant native to the Caucasus region, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant's bulbs are said to contain various compounds with potential health benefits. In traditional medicine, the plant is believed to have a diuretic effect and is used to treat conditions such as kidney stones and urinary tract infections. Its antiseptic properties also make it useful for wound healing and skin infections. Further research is needed to fully understand and confirm the plant's medicinal uses.

side_effects: Tulipa biebersteiniana is a lesser-known species of tulip, but there is limited information on its specific side effects. intro: Tulipa biebersteiniana, also known as the Lily of the Field or Golden Tulip, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Tulipa genus, characterized by its vibrant yellow flowers and narrow, lance-shaped leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Tulipa biebersteiniana include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are tea and infusion, often used to relieve coughs, sore throats, and other respiratory symptoms.
